title: John Deere owners will get the right to repair equipment under FTC settlement
titleZh: 根据联邦贸易委员会和解协议，约翰迪尔所有者将获得维修设备的权利
author: djoldman
relevanceReason: 该帖子涉及约翰迪尔（John Deere）与FTC达成维修权和解协议，要求公司向设备所有者和独立维修店提供诊断和维修工具，这与科技产品维修权、软件限制及反垄断问题密切相关。
summaryRaw: It looks like John Deere owners can soon feel free to fix their own machines.The Federal Trade Commission and attorneys general from several states secured a right-to-repair settlement Wednesday with agriculture equipment giant Deere & Co. — commonly known as John Deere — that requires the company to let farmers and independent shops fix their own equipment.The Illinois-based manufacturer has faced complaints for years for withholding the software needed for repairs and forcing customers to use authorized dealers instead of independent ones. This marks the second right-to-repair settlement Deere has reached this year, following a separate $99 million class-action settlement with farmers in April. Though the class-action compensated consumers, the FTC’s settlement instead requires Deere to make its repair services available to equipment owners and independent shops.The FTC and attorneys general from Arizona, Illinois, Michigan, Minnesota and Wisconsin brought the antitrust lawsuit in January 2025, arguing that Deere had illegally restricted farmers and independent shops that might otherwise service them from repairing farm equipment such as tractors. Deere also makes engines and equipment for forestry, landscaping and construction. Under the order filed in Illinois, Deere will now be required to make diagnostic and repair tools available to equipment owners and independent repair shops, not only its own network of authorized dealers. It also prevents Deere dealers from retaliating against equipment owners or repair shops who choose to fix their own equipment instead of paying for Deere’s services. The order is headed to Judge Iain D. Johnston for his approval.

“For too long, Arizona farmers and independent mechanics have been at the mercy of Deere’s monopoly over repair tools, forced to wait — and pay — for authorized dealers just to fix broken tractors and other equipment,” Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes said in a statement Wednesday. Deere must pay $1 million collectively to the five states for antitrust enforcement costs and will be subject to strict compliance oversight for the next 10 years. In the complaint, the FTC argued that Deere provides a service software tool to authorized dealers but does not provide the full version to equipment owners or independent shops. Deere had said the lawsuit was baseless, denied that its distribution of service tools was anticompetitive and argued that it could not monopolize services since it does not directly provide them.
Sign up for Morning Wire: Our flagship newsletter breaks down the biggest headlines of the day.

Deere maintained its commitment to independent repair in a statement Wednesday, adding that the agreement with the FTC reinforces its innovation of more flexible repair options.“This is good news for our customers and for the future of how Deere equipment is supported,” said Denver Caldwell, vice president of aftermarket and customer support.Right-to-repair has become an increasingly common issue over the years, especially for tech products, with consumers complaining that even simple repairs can only be done by company-authorized dealers.
summaryZh: 美国联邦贸易委员会（FTC）与多州总检察长达成和解，要求约翰迪尔允许农民和独立维修店自行维修设备。
约翰迪尔此前被指控通过限制软件工具分发，强制用户依赖授权经销商进行维修，涉嫌垄断行为。
此次和解是约翰迪尔今年第二次就维修权问题达成协议，此前已支付9900万美元集体诉讼赔偿。
highlightsZh: 维修权运动在科技产品领域日益受到关注，消费者抱怨简单维修也需依赖授权经销商。
约翰迪尔需向设备所有者和独立维修店提供完整的诊断和维修工具，打破授权经销商垄断。
和解协议禁止经销商对选择自行维修的用户或维修店进行报复。
约翰迪尔将支付100万美元用于五州反垄断执法成本，并接受为期10年的严格合规监督。
维修权问题不仅限于农业设备，还涉及科技产品、汽车等多个领域。

comments.author: Cider9986
comments.textRawHtml:
Shout out to Louis Rossmann for doing a ton of work on Right to repair.

He started a website called Consumer Rights Wiki to document anti-consumer practices.

https://consumerrights.wiki/w/Main_Page

He's also involved with FULU Foundation which has a bounty of 25k to get Ring cameras working without Amazon's servers.

https://bounties.fulu.org/bounties/ring-video-doorbells
comments.textZhHtml:
赞扬 Louis Rossmann 在维修权方面所做的大量工作。

他创建了一个名为 Consumer Rights Wiki 的网站来记录反消费者行为。

https://consumerrights.wiki/w/Main_Page

他还参与了 FULU 基金会，该基金会悬赏 25,000 美元让 Ring 相机在不使用的情况下正常工作亚马逊的服务器。

https://bounties.fulu.org/bounties/ring-video-doorbells

children.author: srejk
children.textRawHtml: I appreciate everything he stands for - he's on the right side of just about every issue. I just wish he could make more succinct and effective videos.
children.textZhHtml: 我欣赏他所代表的一切 - 他几乎在所有问题上都站在正确的一边。我只是希望他能制作出更加简洁有效的视频。

children.author: darksim905
children.textRawHtml: You really want more succinct videos than what he already makes? Although they may seem long in a modern day where people have the attention span of a gnat -- his videos are fantastic and to the point. He literally ignores a lot more complexities and drama surrounding a lot of issues. Does he ramble sometimes? Maybe, but the talks so fast at times that the video is over before I've even gotten half way through with my own research. And this is from someone who has followed him since he posted videos on spinning on your own VoIP server and when he was still based in NYC.
children.textZhHtml: 您真的想要比他已经制作的视频更简洁的视频吗？尽管在当今人们的注意力集中时间有限的今天，这些视频可能看起来很长，但他的视频非常棒，而且切题。他确实忽略了围绕许多问题的更多复杂性和戏剧性。他有时会胡言乱语吗？也许吧，但有时谈话速度太快，以至于我自己的研究还没完成一半，视频就结束了。这是来自自从他在您自己的 VoIP 服务器上发布有关旋转的视频以及他还在纽约时就一直关注他的人。

children.author: Zancarius
children.textRawHtml:
"An account of human suffering" (assuming I remember the approximate title) is one of his earlier videos that sticks out in my mind and one I'll never forget. It was a tongue-in-cheek nod to the fact board diagnostics for hardware that has obviously been abused by the end user is a tedious and often thankless process.

I learned a LOT from watching his videos, and I don't even do board repair! Psychology, business, the human condition...
children.textZhHtml:
“人类苦难的叙述”（假设我记得大概的标题）是他早期的视频之一，在我的脑海中挥之不去，我永远不会忘记。这是对硬件主板诊断显然被最终用户滥用这一事实的半开玩笑的认可，这是一个乏味且常常吃力不讨好的过程。

我从观看他的视频中学到了很多东西，而且我什至不进行主板维修！心理学、商业、人类状况……

children.author: cgyvbunji
children.textRawHtml: Personally I think if he did that people wouldn't pay attention. My experience is that when you complain to people about digital rights they just glaze over unless you walk them through all the ins and outs and the implications.
children.textZhHtml: 我个人认为，如果他这样做，人们就不会注意到。我的经验是，当你向人们抱怨数字版权时，除非你向他们介绍所有的来龙去脉及其影响，否则他们只会呆呆地看着。

children.author: forinti
children.textRawHtml: It's an uphill battle to inform the public. Most people just don't understand and don't care to understand the issues until it actually hits them, such as with the farmers who bought JD.
children.textZhHtml: 向公众通报情况是一场艰苦的战斗。大多数人只是不理解，也不愿意理解这些问题，直到问题真正发生在他们身上，例如购买京东的农民。

children.author: wickedsight
children.textRawHtml: I'm one of the people who mostly ignores his videos due to their length and the 'old man angry ranting about things' vibe they give me.
children.textZhHtml: 我是那些大多会忽略他的视频的人之一，因为它们的长度以及它们给我的“老人对事情愤怒咆哮”的氛围。

children.author: FatherOfCurses
children.textRawHtml: Everyone should be as angry as him, then companies wouldn't keep screwing us over.
children.textZhHtml: 每个人都应该像他一样愤怒，这样公司们就不会一直坑我们了。

children.author: tsumnia
children.textRawHtml: "I'm as mad as hell and I'm not going to take this anymore!"
children.textZhHtml: “我气得要死，再也忍不下去了！”

children.author: sneakymichael
children.textRawHtml:
Ditto. I love his ideology, and have watched a few of his videos over the years, but his lengthy roundabout style of delivery doesn't keep me engaged.

Super glad he does what he does / is who he is though.
children.textZhHtml:
同感。我很认同他的理念，这些年也看过他的一些视频，但他那种冗长迂回的表达方式实在让我提不起劲。

不过，我还是非常高兴他能坚持做他所做的事，做他自己。

children.author: Shocka1
children.textRawHtml:
Almost every conversation I've had over the years goes two ways: "Why do you care so much about <Flock, Ring Cameras, big tech name>? If you have nothing to hide, why are you so concerned?"

Or:

"Why do you care so much about <Flock, Ring Cameras, big tech name>? We already walk around with cell phones in our pockets 24/7."

I used to be more passionate about persuading people about privacy, but I've pretty much given up. I've come to the conclusion that the majority of the population doesn't care what is happening around them as long as they have internet access and their iPhone.
children.textZhHtml:
多年来，我经历的几乎每一次对话都分为两种情况： “你为什么这么在意<Flock、Ring摄像头、大型科技公司名称>？如果你没做什么见不得人的事，干嘛这么担心？”

或者：

“你为什么这么在意<Flock、Ring摄像头、大型科技公司名称>？反正我们现在都是24小时手机不离身。”

过去我曾更热衷于劝说人们关注隐私问题，但现在我基本放弃了。我得出的结论是，只要还能上网、还能用iPhone，大多数人根本不在乎周围正在发生什么。

children.author: archontes
children.textRawHtml:
My response is typically, "I have literally everything to hide. I don't want you to know my middle name. I don't want you to know what I had for breakfast. It's none of anyone's business."

And weirdly, the response I get is, "Oh yeah, fair."
children.textZhHtml:
我通常的回答是：“我简直有无数事情要隐瞒。我不想让你知道我的中间名，也不想让你知道我早餐吃了什么。这跟任何人都没关系。”

而奇怪的是，我得到的回应却是：“哦，也是，说得在理。”

children.author: dataflow
children.textRawHtml:
I share his videos here but nobody seems to see them and/or care enough to vote; all I get is crickets. It's baffling to me. Examples:

- https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48802162

- https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48395520

- https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47043208
children.textZhHtml:
我在这里分享他的视频，但似乎没有人看到它们和/或关心投票；我得到的只是蟋蟀。这让我很困惑。示例：

- https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48802162

- https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48395520

- https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47043208

children.author: throw2ih020
children.textRawHtml: HN audience prefers text to video; videos rarely do well here compared to other sites.
children.textZhHtml: HN的受众更喜欢文字而不是视频；与其他网站相比，视频在这里很少能取得好成绩。

children.author: spacedcowboy
children.textRawHtml:
This. I never watch video posts. I can skim-read a text site in seconds to see if it's worth a real read. I can't do that with video, and there's always some ads in it, whether the author put them there or not. I hate ads.

And it's not just HN either, video is a very poor substitute for a well written article. Video can augment a well-written article, but it can't replace it.
children.textZhHtml:
确实如此。我从来不看视频帖子。我可以在几秒钟内略读一个文字网站，看看是否值得仔细阅读。但视频做不到这一点，而且里面总是有广告，不管是不是作者自己加的。我讨厌广告。

而且这不仅仅是HN的问题，视频根本无法很好地替代一篇好文章。视频可以补充一篇好文章，但无法取代它。

children.author: pchristensen
children.textRawHtml:
If you have YouTube Premium, you can use their Gemini AI to summarize videos. I've "consumed" tons more videos since I discovered it, since I can get a good idea from the summary if it's worth actually watching the video.

My overall minutes of YT have gone down, but I probably interacts with 2-3x more videos. Just like skimming on HN!
children.textZhHtml:
如果你有YouTube Premium，你可以用他们的Gemini AI来总结视频。自从发现这个功能后，我“消费”的视频数量大幅增加，因为通过摘要我就能大致判断这个视频是否值得真正去看。

我在YouTube上花费的总时长减少了，但我互动的视频数量可能增加了2到3倍。就像在HN上略读一样！

children.author: bfdm
children.textRawHtml: If you're Google you could transcribe and summarize all videos on upload for easier consumption, but then you lose your ad revenue so...
children.textZhHtml: 如果你是谷歌，你大可以在视频上传时就自动转录并生成摘要，让用户更轻松地获取内容，但那样就会损失广告收入，所以……

children.author: gosub100
children.textRawHtml: Posting here for anyone who might not know this: you can click and hold on most YouTube videos and they will play at 2x. I find that to be a good test at whether the host is communicating effectively. If I can understand them perfectly clear at 2X they are talking too slowly.
children.textZhHtml: 发在这里给可能不知道的人提个醒：在大多数YouTube视频上长按屏幕，它们就会以2倍速播放。我发现这是测试主持人表达是否有效的一个好方法。如果我在2倍速下能听得清清楚楚，那就说明他们说话太慢了。

children.author: bombcar
children.textRawHtml:
Then you get things like the ATP (I can't bring myself to say ATP podcast because La Brea Tar Pits and all that) where two hosts are fine at 1.5x and the third becomes a chipmunk.

Maybe they can make Overcast have dynamic speed based on speaker.
children.textZhHtml:
然后你就会遇到ATP这种情况（我实在说不出口“ATP播客”，因为拉布雷亚沥青坑之类的梗）两位主持人在1.5倍速下听着还行，第三位就变成花栗鼠了。

也许他们能让Overcast根据说话人动态调整播放速度。

children.author: sneakymichael
children.textRawHtml: Who of the three do you feel speaks much faster naturally?
children.textZhHtml: 这三位里，你觉得谁天生语速快得多？
